nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving and still freshwater habitats in tropical and subtropical Asia; the 'Taiwan' cultivar is traced to southern Taiwan and is found in shallow, sun-exposed slow-flow waters.

avoidWith: vegetarian plecos that shred soft leaves

summary: Taiwan Floating Heart (Nymphoides hydrophylla) is a rosette plant from tropical Asia with heart-shaped, slightly crinkled leaves on slender stems. The 'Taiwan' cultivar tends to stay more submerged than other Nymphoides, producing a dense cluster of underwater leaves rather than an immediate surface canopy.
careNotes:
Nymphoides hydrophylla 'Taiwan' is the form of N. hydrophylla most commonly encountered in the aquarium trade, and is best known for producing many bright, translucent light-green submerged leaves on short stems rather than a heavy floating canopy — a habit that makes it considerably better suited to the enclosed aquarium than many other Nymphoides species. It is closely related to the North American Banana Plant (Nymphoides aquatica) but lacks that species' characteristic banana-shaped tuber storage organs at the base of the stem. The plant is generally considered beginner-friendly across a wide range of conditions. The rosette has been reported at roughly 15–30 cm tall and 10–15 cm wide once established, with individual leaves around 3–6 cm across. It has been reported to tolerate temperatures from approximately 20–30 °C, with most sources pointing to 22–28 °C as the practical aquarium range; pH 6.0–7.5 is the common recommendation, though tolerance has been reported to extend to around pH 8; soft to moderately hard water (gH approximately 3–15, kH approximately 6–12) is generally fine.

This plant is a strong root feeder — a nutrient-rich substrate (such as aquasoil) or regular root tabs is recommended, with water-column fertilizers serving as a useful supplement rather than the primary source of nutrition. Lighting interacts visibly with leaf morphology: under medium-to-high light the plant tends to stay compact, with shorter stems and smaller leaves (around 3–4 cm across); under low light, leaves enlarge (up to around 6 cm) and stems stretch upward, often sending floating lily-pad leaves to the surface. CO2 supplementation is not required but tends to produce denser, more controlled growth — without it, the plant may reach the surface more readily. Growth is generally medium-to-fast under good conditions. Thinning older and surface leaves periodically keeps the rosette tidy and prevents shading of nearby plants.

Propagation is by adventitious plantlets (small offshoots that develop naturally on older leaves or at leaf nodes) — trim a mature leaf with a portion of stem, allow it to form roots and a new plantlet, then replant. Side-shoot and runner propagation also works once a clump is well established. The plant is best placed in the midground to background of the aquarium; in smaller tanks a single specimen may be sufficient to avoid crowding.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica species page provided origin (southern Taiwan), family/genus identity, rosette habit (15–30+ cm tall, 10–15 cm wide), easy care, fast growth, and a low-light tolerance rating with medium CO2 demand. Tropica's search landing page did not deep-link; the species detail URL was confirmed via search.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Confirmed pH 6.0–7.5, adult height ~10–15 cm (4–6 inches), midground-to-background placement, easy-to-moderate care, CO2 not necessary but beneficial, runner/side-shoot propagation, and strong root-feeder behaviour. Light-intensity vs leaf-size trade-off (low light → stretching, surface leaves; brighter light → compact form) cross-confirmed.

additional.name: Gensou Aquascaping
additional.notes: Provided numeric water-parameter ranges that the primary and Buce Plant pages omitted: temperature 22–30 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, gH 3–15, lighting target 50–80 PAR (compact form needs 100+), CO2 optional at 15–30 ppm, growth medium-to-fast, height 15–30 cm with 3–6 cm leaves. Native range stated as tropical/subtropical Asia.

additional.name: Light Fish
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters: temperature ~20–27 °C (68–80 °F), pH 6–8, kH 6–12; described as beginner-friendly and fast-growing; native range stated as southern Taiwan with broader Nymphoides spread across Asia and Africa.

propagation: adventitious plantlets from older leaves (trim leaf with stem, allow to root)
side shoots and runners from established crown
